summary: Cleaned up game-mode struct usage by replacing ad hoc gm structs and placeholder members with known mn/types.h structs where applicable, especially PlayerInitData, StartMeleeRules, StartMeleeData, and VsModeData. The PR also added supporting gm matches that exercise the newly clarified fields, producing 14 new matched functions and improvements in two larger unmatched functions according to the decomp.dev bot report.

change_classification.primary_type: types-structs cleanup with supporting decomp matches

what_changed: Replaced placeholder/ad hoc gm struct access with known mn/types.h structures, including struct PlayerInitData, struct StartMeleeRules, struct StartMeleeData, and struct VsModeData.
Updated lbl_8046B6A0 layout in gm/types.h so offset 0x24C8 is represented as struct StartMeleeRules x24C8 instead of placeholder fields.

Reworked mn/types.h fields from descriptive-but-uncertain names such as character_kind, slot_type, flags1, item_mask, damage_ratio, and game_speed into offset-based xN and xN_bM names with comments where meaning is known.
Added explicit bitfields for StartMeleeRules flags x0_0 through x5_7 and PlayerInitData flags xC_b0 through xD_b7.

Implemented previously unmatched gm_1601 functions: gm_8016795C initializes one PlayerInitData, gm_80167A14 initializes six PlayerInitData entries, gm_80167A64 initializes StartMeleeRules defaults, gm_80167B50 initializes VsModeData, and gm_80168FC4 loads announcer audio.

Implemented or partially matched gm_16AE functions around melee setup and flow, including gm_8016D800, fn_8016D8AC, fn_8016DCC0, fn_8016E730, fn_8016C7D0, and several OnEnter functions shown by the bot report.

Refined many gm_1601.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures, including functions taking CharacterKind, HSD_GObj*, PlayerInitData*, StartMeleeRules*, StartMeleeData*, and VsModeData*.

Changed gm_8016AE50 to return struct StartMeleeRules* and updated accessors such as gm_8016AE80, gm_8016AEA4, gm_8016AEB8, gm_8016AEC8, gm_8016B004, and related flag helpers to read fields through lbl_8046B6A0.x24C8.
Updated gmvsmelee APIs so gm_801A5244 returns VsModeData* and gm_801A5618 takes MinorScene*, VsModeData*, and int.
Adjusted interface and library headers/call sites affected by StartMeleeRules typing, including ifstatus, iftime, and lbbgflash declarations.
smart_moves: Used newly matched gm code as regression coverage for struct layout: initialization and setup routines write many fields, making incorrect offsets or bitfield packing more likely to break matching.
Converted placeholder struct members to real shared mn/types.h structures rather than duplicating game-mode-specific approximations.
Kept uncertain member names offset-based while preserving semantic comments for known meanings, reducing the risk of overconfident naming while still documenting discoveries.
Introduced typed function prototypes in headers alongside implementation matches, allowing type information to propagate across gm, mn, if, lb, and player-related call sites.
Used StartMeleeRules x24C8 inside lbl_8046B6A0 to align global game-state accesses with the same struct used by StartMeleeData.
Added a pragma dont_inline wrapper around gm_8016B558, indicating matching-sensitive compiler behavior was preserved explicitly.
decomp_lessons: When a global state blob embeds a known data structure, prefer representing the embedded range as that known struct and update accessor functions to prove offsets.
Large struct cleanups are safer when paired with small matches that initialize, copy, or consume the struct; these functions serve as practical layout tests.
For uncertain fields, offset-based names such as x24C8, x20, x2C, xC_b0, and x4_7 are acceptable and often better than premature semantic names.
Bitfield-heavy game configuration structs should be modeled with explicit bit positions once matching evidence identifies access patterns.
Header prototype cleanup can unlock matches because typed parameters eliminate casts/placeholders and make member access express the intended layout.
Cross-system structs such as StartMeleeRules affect gm, mn, if, lb, player, stage, camera, and audio code; expect cleanup to require include and declaration updates across subsystems.
naming_conventions: Use offset-style names for uncertain struct fields: x0, x1, xA, x10, x24C8.
Use bitfield names in the form xBYTE_BIT, e.g. xC_b0, xD_b7, x0_3, x4_7.
Keep comments for known semantics while retaining offset names when confidence is incomplete, e.g. x0 // character kind, x1 // slot type, x20 // item mask, x34 // game speed.
Use real shared struct type names where known: PlayerInitData, StartMeleeRules, StartMeleeData, VsModeData.
Preserve address-based function names when no better name is established, e.g. gm_8016795C, fn_8016D8AC, gm_8016AE50.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched struct initialization functions by using memzero followed by exact field stores for PlayerInitData and StartMeleeRules defaults.
Modeled bitfields explicitly to match flag reads/writes against packed bytes in StartMeleeRules and PlayerInitData.
Used typed member access through lbl_8046B6A0.x24C8 instead of raw placeholder offsets to preserve codegen while improving readability.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in gm_8016D800, showing stack-shape preservation was needed for the match.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_8016B558 to keep inlining behavior matching-sensitive.
Kept local static anonymous structs for lbl_803D5620 and lbl_803D5630 in gm_16AE.c to match data references used during StartMeleeData setup.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 33.67%, +0.05%, +2036 bytes.

Bot report listed 14 new matches, including fn_8016E730, gm_80167A64, gm_8016EC28_OnEnter, gm_8016795C, gm_8016D800, gm_8016E934_OnEnter, gm_80167B50, gm_8016EBC0_OnEnter, gm_801A5618, gm_80167A14, gm_80168FC4, fn_80171AD4, gm_801A5598_OnInit, and fn_8016C7D0.
Bot report showed improvements in unmatched functions fn_8016D8AC to 92.34% and fn_8016DCC0 to 85.21%.
Bot report also noted small regressions in unmatched ifstatus functions ifStatus_802F6EA4, ifStatus_802F7034, and ifStatus_802F7134, each by 4 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename PlayerInitData x-fields to semantic names after this layout cleanup.
Search for follow-up matches to fn_8016D8AC and fn_8016DCC0 after their partial improvements in PR 1678.
Check whether the ifstatus regressions from the bot report were accepted as harmless or fixed in a later PR.
Look for subsequent changes to StartMeleeRules bitfields x0_0 through x5_7 and callbacks x38, x44, x48, x4C.
Search gm_16AE users of lbl_8046B6A0.x24C8 to validate or rename remaining StartMeleeRules fields.
